description: Enforcement specification for component governance lifecycle. Every rule references actual registry fields and Phase 5 artifacts. This is NOT aspirational—it is an operative control mechanism.

componentLifecycle.rule: No component enters registry without passing all phases. governancePhase field on each component (ai-component-registry.json > components[].governancePhase) tracks current phase. Only values: 'proposed', 'specification', 'humanApproval', 'implementation', 'documentation', 'released', 'deprecated'.
componentLifecycle.enforcement: ds-registry-sync skill enforces phase transitions. Cannot advance phase without passing all gates.

interruptPolicy.perComponentOverrides: Each component's interruptPolicy field (ai-component-registry.json > components[].interruptPolicy) can add component-specific interrupt rules. Example: ds-button.interruptPolicy.alwaysInterrupt = [].
interruptPolicy.enforcement: Agent must check component.interruptPolicy before executing any operation. If operation is in alwaysInterrupt list, halt and request human approval via governance record.

steps.name: Search Registry
steps.action: When component not found or rejected, search ai-component-registry.json > components for closest alternative by name similarity or purpose match

steps.name: If Found
steps.action: Use found component. Log correction to state audit trail.

steps.name: If Not Found
steps.action: Search ai-component-registry.json for closest alternative in existing DS

steps.name: Report to Governance
steps.action: Create governance record with: missing component name, use case, recommended alternative, status=PENDING_HUMAN_APPROVAL

steps.name: Use Alternative
steps.action: Use recommended alternative (ai-component-registry.json > components[name==recommendedAlternativeName]) until governance approves new component proposal
steps.constraint: Cannot use rejected component. Must use released alternative or request new component.

rejectionProtocol.perComponentFallbacks: Each component defines rejectionHandling (ai-component-registry.json > components[].rejectionHandling). fallbackComponent field maps to name of component to use if this component is rejected. Example: ds-button.rejectionHandling.fallbackComponent = 'ds-primary-button'.
rejectionProtocol.enforcement: When component.rejectionHandling.reportToGovernance=true, MUST report rejection. Create governance audit record before proceeding.

statePolicy.enforcement: All state changes to components or registry must be logged to deltaLog. No silent modifications. Audit trail is immutable.

reasoningPolicy.enforcement: All reasoning traces must be stored in plaintext and accessible via audit trail. Cannot redact reasoning. Design decisions must be explainable.

rules.name: Dual hover selectors
rules.description: Every :hover pseudo-class MUST have a corresponding .hover class selector
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks component CSS for :hover without matching .hover class
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Focus-visible ring
rules.description: Every element that can receive keyboard focus MUST have focus-visible styling with 2px ring
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks for :focus-visible with box-shadow or outline
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Disabled hover suppression
rules.description: Disabled elements MUST NOT show hover visual feedback
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks .disabled:hover states are suppressed
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Hover overlay pattern
rules.description: Hover on filled/inverted surfaces MUST use ::before with currentColor + opacity or color-mix()
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill validates hover overlay implementation pattern
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Compound states for inputs
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks input component state combinations
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Box-shadow borders
rules.description: Outlined borders use box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px, not CSS border
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill detects CSS border properties on outlined elements
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Explicit typography
rules.description: Every text element MUST have font-family, font-size, and color explicitly set
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks all text elements for complete typography tokens
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Disabled color propagation
rules.description: When parent is disabled, ALL child text elements MUST switch to --content-disabled
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks disabled state color inheritance on all text children
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Truncation trio
rules.description: Text that can overflow MUST have text-overflow, white-space, and overflow together
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks all overflowable text has complete truncation properties
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Logical positioning
rules.description: Never use left/right. Always use inset-inline-start/inset-inline-end
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill detects physical directional positioning properties
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Logical border-radius
rules.description: Never use physical directional border-radius. Always use logical properties
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill detects physical border-radius like border-radius: 8px 0 0 8px
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Logical spacing
rules.description: Never use margin-left/right or padding-left/right. Always use logical variants
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill detects physical spacing properties
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Logical text alignment
rules.description: Never use text-align: left/right. Always use text-align: start/end
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill detects physical text alignment values
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Reduced motion
rules.description: Components with movement animations MUST respect prefers-reduced-motion
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) on animated components
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: No raw values
rules.description: No hex colors, rgb(), or raw px. Use tokens exclusively
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill scans CSS for hardcoded colors, rgb, rgba, raw pixel values
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Dynamic token panel
rules.description: Token panel function MUST accept config, branch per emphasis, add state-specific tokens
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks component token panel implementation against pattern
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: State class pattern
rules.description: Components MUST define .hover, .focused, .error, .disabled classes alongside pseudo-classes
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks all state pseudo-classes have matching class selectors
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: Wrapper state propagation
rules.description: For components with labels/helpers, use wrapper class to cascade state
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks wrapper elements properly propagate state to children
rules.failure: Component fails audit. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

rules.name: RTL directional icons
rules.description: Directional icons MUST mirror in RTL via [dir='rtl'] selector with transform: scaleX(-1)
rules.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks directional icons (chevron, arrow, caret) for RTL handling
rules.failure: Component fails audit if RTL icon mirroring not implemented. Cannot advance to documentation phase.

complianceRules.validator: ds-validator skill runs against ds-library.html, produces pass/fail per rule per component. Output: audit-report.json with pass/fail matrix.
complianceRules.passRequirement: Component must pass U1-U19 with score of 90+ to advance from implementation to documentation phase.

registrySync.rule: All JSON registry files are generated from ds-library.html. Never hand-edited. Same input = same output (deterministic). Registry is authoritative source of truth.
registrySync.enforcement: Before any component operation, validate that component exists in ai-component-registry.json (components[].name match). If registry mismatch detected, regenerate from ds-library.html. No stale registries allowed.

approvalGates.rule: Design lead or product owner must explicitly approve. No automation.
approvalGates.evidenceRequired: approval-record.json with approver name, date, signature

approvalGates.rule: Design system team peer reviews specification completeness
approvalGates.evidenceRequired: specification.md complete with all required sections

approvalGates.rule: Human stakeholder must explicitly approve before implementation. This is a hard gate.
approvalGates.evidenceRequired: approval-record.json with timestamp, approver identity, explicit sign-off

approvalGates.rule: Component must pass U1-U19 audit with 90+ score. Code coverage must be 80%+. No hardcoded values.
approvalGates.lookupPath: ds-validator skill output: audit-report.json for component
approvalGates.evidenceRequired: audit-report.json with all U1-U19 rules passed, test-coverage.json with 80%+ coverage

approvalGates.rule: All documentation complete, accurate, linked, examples tested
approvalGates.lookupPath: docs/ folder, registry.json entries, knowledge-graph.json relationships
approvalGates.evidenceRequired: All required doc files exist, registry updated with governancePhase='documentation'

approvalGates.rule: Release manager approves. Documentation live. Package published. No critical bugs.
approvalGates.evidenceRequired: npm package published, git tag created, component.governancePhase='released'

consistencySafeguards.rule: Token architecture must not be broken by new additions
consistencySafeguards.enforcement: Token validator checks new component tokens against ai-token-architecture.json
Before proposal accepted, search ai-component-registry.json > components[].name for exact match

consistencySafeguards.rule: Interaction patterns must be consistent across similar components
consistencySafeguards.enforcement: ds-validator skill checks interaction state patterns match existing components in same category
WideBot=WCAG AAA audit pass, Hulul=WCAG AA audit pass. Check component.accessibility field in registry.
Proposer must document why composition of existing patterns is insufficient. ai-knowledge-graph.json searched for related patterns.

consistencySafeguards.rule: All visual specs must use design tokens
consistencySafeguards.enforcement: U15 audit rule: no hardcoded values. 100% compliance required.

consistencySafeguards.rule: Component names must follow naming convention
consistencySafeguards.enforcement: Registry validator checks name format: ds-[category]-[variant] in lowercase with hyphens

phases.name: Proposal
phases.description: New component need is identified and formally proposed

requiredFields.name: Component name in lowercase-hyphenated format (ds-xxx-yyy)
requiredFields.purpose: One-line description of what the component does
requiredFields.category: One of: general, containers, inputs, navigation, data-display, composed
requiredFields.dependencies: List of existing components this depends on
requiredFields.justification: Why this cannot be achieved by composing existing components
requiredFields.targetProducts: Which products or features need this

validationRules: Name must not conflict with ai-component-registry.json > components[].name
Must demonstrate that existing components cannot satisfy need through composition
Layer assignment must respect dependency rules
Must identify at least one target product
Category must be valid

phases.name: Specification

requiredFields.api: Complete prop interface with TypeScript types
requiredFields.states: Default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, success

validationRules: API must be minimal and expressive
All states must be testable
Accessibility requirements must include keyboard navigation
Variants must follow naming conventions
Design tokens must be used (not hardcoded)
Must pass U1-U19 audit template requirements

phases.name: Human Approval
phases.description: Specification is explicitly approved by human stakeholder before implementation

requiredFields.designGoalAlignment: How this aligns with design system goals

validationRules: Approver must have authority to approve
Approval must be explicit (not implicit)
Specification must not have unresolved comments
All previous phase validation must pass
Cannot proceed without explicit sign-off

approval.criteria: Human has reviewed and explicitly approves proceeding
approval.evidenceRequired: Recorded approval with timestamp and identity

phases.name: Implementation
phases.description: Component is implemented in code following specification, with tests and documentation
validationRules: Implementation must match specification exactly
All props and variants from spec must be implemented
No hardcoded values - all use design tokens
Test coverage must be 80%+
TypeScript types must match specification
Keyboard navigation must work per accessibility spec
Must pass U1-U19 audit (90+ score)
Code must follow style guide from existing components

review.criteria: U1-U19 audit passes all checks, tests pass, coverage above 80%

phases.name: Documentation
phases.description: All documentation is written and integrated into design system knowledge base
validationRules: All documentation must be clear and complete
Code examples must be functional and tested
API reference must match implementation exactly
Content guidelines must include visual examples
Accessibility documentation must reference WCAG success criteria
Registry entry must include governancePhase='documentation'
All documentation must be internally linked
Roadmap must be updated

review.criteria: All required documentation complete, accurate, examples tested

phases.name: Release
phases.description: Component is packaged, versioned, and released

requiredFields.npmPackage: Published to npm under design system package
requiredFields.figmaLibrary: Design component added to Figma library

validationRules: All documentation must be live before release
npm package must include TypeScript types
Changelog must be complete and accurate
Version number must follow semantic versioning (per CLAUDE.md)
Figma design library must be updated
All tests must pass
No known critical bugs
Release notes must explain what changed
Component.governancePhase must be updated to 'released' in ai-component-registry.json

review.criteria: All documentation live, package published, no critical bugs

phases.name: Deprecation
phases.description: Component is phased out when replaced, with clear migration path

requiredFields.sunsetDate: Date after which component no longer supported

validationRules: Replacement component must be fully documented and released
Migration guide must be specific with code examples
Support window must be minimum 6 months
Users must be notified 3+ months before sunset
All references in docs must be updated
Component.governancePhase must be 'deprecated' in ai-component-registry.json

review.criteria: Replacement ready, migration guide complete, users notified

roles.proposer: Any team member or AI agent who identifies a component need
roles.spec-writer: Design system copilot agent or design system team member
roles.approver: Human stakeholder with decision authority (design lead, product owner)
roles.implementer: Frontend engineer or design system copilot agent
roles.documentation-team: Technical writer or design system team member
roles.release-manager: Design system team lead or product manager
roles.automated-audit: ds-validator skill that validates implementation against U1-U19
roles.design-lead: Design system lead with approval authority
roles.product-owner: Product owner for the product using the component

enforcementMechanisms.name: Registry Sync
enforcementMechanisms.action: Validate component exists in ai-component-registry.json. If mismatch, regenerate from ds-library.html.
enforcementMechanisms.consequence: Operation blocked until registry is current

enforcementMechanisms.name: U1-U19 Audit
enforcementMechanisms.action: Run audit-report.json generation against component CSS/JS. Generate pass/fail per rule.
enforcementMechanisms.consequence: Component cannot advance to documentation without 90+ score

enforcementMechanisms.name: Interrupt Check
enforcementMechanisms.consequence: If operation listed, halt and request human approval via governance record

enforcementMechanisms.name: Rejection Fallback
enforcementMechanisms.trigger: When component not found or rejected
enforcementMechanisms.action: Search registry per rejectionProtocol steps. Use fallback component or report to governance.
enforcementMechanisms.consequence: If reported, component cannot be used until governance approves proposal

enforcementMechanisms.name: State Audit Trail
enforcementMechanisms.trigger: Every state change to component or registry
enforcementMechanisms.action: Log to deltaLog: timestamp, actor, operation, path, previousValue, newValue, reason
enforcementMechanisms.consequence: All changes tracked permanently. Immutable audit trail.

rules.statement: No component may be created or added to the registry without explicit human approval in the humanApproval phase
rules.enforcement: Agent must verify component.governancePhase >= 'humanApproval' before creating registry entry

rules.statement: All component changes must be tracked in version control and audit trail
rules.enforcement: All operations must log to deltaLog. No silent modifications.
rules.consequence: BLOCK: if deltaLog entry not created, operation fails

rules.statement: No breaking API changes in released components without major version bump and deprecation period
rules.enforcement: Release manager must verify version follows semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH per CLAUDE.md)
rules.consequence: BLOCK: release rejected if breaking changes not documented

rules.statement: All styling must use design tokens. No hardcoded values permitted.
rules.enforcement: U15 audit rule: ds-validator scans for hardcoded colors, rgb(), rgba(), raw px values
rules.consequence: BLOCK: component fails audit, cannot advance phases

rules.statement: All components must have 80%+ code coverage with passing tests
rules.enforcement: Implementation phase review requires test-coverage.json with coverage >= 80%
rules.consequence: BLOCK: cannot advance to documentation without test coverage

rules.statement: All components must meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards
rules.enforcement: Accessibility audit check during implementation review
rules.consequence: BLOCK: cannot release component if accessibility audit fails

rules.statement: All components must have complete documentation before release
rules.enforcement: Documentation phase gate requires all required doc files to exist
rules.consequence: BLOCK: cannot release without complete documentation

rules.statement: All components must have complete TypeScript type definitions
rules.enforcement: Implementation phase requires types.ts matching specification interface
rules.consequence: BLOCK: cannot release without complete types

lookupPathReference.description: Quick reference for all lookup paths used in governance enforcement

paths.use: Check for component name duplicates before accepting proposal
Determine current phase of component lifecycle
Check component-specific interrupt rules before operation
Determine fallback component when rejection occurs
Check global interrupt rules (localFilesystemAccess, coreDsFileModification, etc.)
Follow rejection protocol steps when component not found
Enforce mandatory state logging and audit trail requirements
Enforce plaintext storage of reasoning traces and visibility rules
Find all released components for fallback search
Validate component implementation against all universal rules
Authoritative source for all registry generation

implementationNote: This file is OPERATIVE. Every field references actual code paths, actual registry structures, and actual Phase 5 artifacts. This is not aspirational—it is an enforcement specification that governs all component operations. The ds-registry-sync, ds-validator, and agent governance engine tools use these rules as operative constraints. All component lifecycle transitions are validated against these rules before proceeding.
